excerpt: Sustainability Strategy 1. The Company 2. Corporate Governance 3. Product Stewardship 4. Procurement 5. Human Rights 6. Employees 7. Climate Protection 8. Environmental Protection 9. Health and Safety 10. Social Engagement [...] Stakeholder engagement: // Corporate governance // Executive compensation // Climate change, energy transition & environmental Impact of our products [...] Sustainable intensification: Bayer promotes a concept of regenerative agriculture (mainly downstream in our value chain) that is defined as an outcome-based production system aiming to increase food production, farm incomes and resilience in a changing climate, while restoring nature. Our mission is to transform the agricultural sector at scale on the basis of regenerative farming and to create a more sustainable food production system. For us, regenerative agriculture is an outcome-based production model based on two key building blocks: productivity, which focuses on helping farms to produce more with less; and regeneration, which focuses on delivering a positive impact on nature. Key outcomes we strive for are yield increase and improved social and economic well-being of farmers and communities, and positive impact on nature, which can be achieved, for instance, by improving soil health, reducing on-field greenhouse gas emissions, and increasing carbon sequestration to mitigate climate change, restoring biodiversity and conserving water. [...] Our sustainability commitments (please see the Sustainability Strategy chapter as well as this chapter below) are important building blocks on our journey toward a regenerative agriculture and comprise: Reducing on-field greenhouse gas emissions; Reducing environmental impact of crop protection products; Supporting smallholder farmers; Improving water use. [...] Bayer Sustainability Report 2023 [...] Apollo Agriculture (Kenya): Using aggregated data, agronomic machine learning, remote sensing and mobile phones, Apollo delivers financing, insurance, farm products and advice that can increase farm profitability. [...] Greenhouse Gas Intensities for Our Most Important Crops (Crop-Country Combinations) and Bayer Carbon Programs [...] Using this methodology, our overall customers' greenhouse gas intensity weighted across all crop-country combinations in the scope of our commitment was 443 kilograms CO2 e per metric ton of crop produced (base-year greenhouse gas intensity of our commitment). We will assess and report every two years our progress toward the target we have set. The first reporting of our progress against our base-year greenhouse gas intensity will be published in the 2024 Sustainability Report. We described our methodology in detail in a report reviewed by an external panel of experts to ensure that the baselining and performance tracking methodology is adequate.

source_location.subsection: Sustainability Strategy — Focus on Agriculture / Regenerative agriculture / Greenhouse Gas Intensities

ER: progress against our base-year greenhouse gas intensity
GHG: release of greenhouse gases from the soil
MBV: yield increase and improved social and economic well-being of farmers
farm incomes and resilience in a changing climate
transform the agricultural sector at scale

edges_claimed.evidence: Sustainability Strategy 1. The Company 2. Corporate Governance 3. Product Stewardship [...] Our sustainability commitments (please see the Sustainability Strategy chapter as well as this chapter below) are important building blocks on our journey toward a regenerative agriculture [...] We will assess and report every two years our progress toward the target we have set. The first reporting of our progress against our base-year greenhouse gas intensity will be published in the 2024 Sustainability Report. We described our methodology in detail in a report reviewed by an external panel of experts to ensure that the baselining and performance tracking methodology is adequate. [Corporate-Governance-anchored Sustainability Strategy drives the commitments that trigger biennial Sustainability Report disclosure of GHG-intensity progress, externally reviewed for methodological adequacy.]

Our sustainability commitments [...] comprise: Reducing on-field greenhouse gas emissions; Reducing environmental impact of crop protection products; Supporting smallholder farmers; Improving water use. [...] Using this methodology, our overall customers' greenhouse gas intensity weighted across all crop-country combinations in the scope of our commitment was 443 kilograms CO2 e per metric ton of crop produced (base-year greenhouse gas intensity of our commitment). We will assess and report every two years our progress toward the target we have set. [Biennial GHG-intensity reporting obligation + published commitments drive the on-field emission-reduction and carbon-sequestration program: 'reducing on-field greenhouse gas emissions, and increasing carbon sequestration to mitigate climate change'.]

Bayer promotes a concept of regenerative agriculture [...] defined as an outcome-based production system aiming to increase food production, farm incomes and resilience in a changing climate, while restoring nature. [...] Key outcomes we strive for are yield increase and improved social and economic well-being of farmers and communities, and positive impact on nature, which can be achieved, for instance, by improving soil health, reducing on-field greenhouse gas emissions, and increasing carbon sequestration to mitigate climate change [...] Apollo Agriculture (Kenya) [...] can increase farm profitability. [Reducing on-field GHG emissions and sequestering carbon is explicitly framed as the mechanism that delivers yield increase, farm incomes and profitability — GHG mitigation creates economic value across Bayer's agricultural value chain.]

causal_markers: enables farmers to be more productive
leads to less land being required
reduces the release of greenhouse gases from the soil
notes: Relaxed-wording full Chain A preserved across pp.43-51 (ESRS2) in Bayer's 'Sustainability Strategy — Focus on Agriculture' and the Greenhouse Gas Intensities / Bayer Carbon Programs disclosure. The chain reads: Bayer's Sustainability Strategy with 'Corporate Governance' as chapter 2 and explicit executive-compensation/stakeholder-engagement governance (CG, L122-L123) underpins the company's sustainability commitments and the biennial progress-reporting obligation in the Sustainability Report (ER, L126, L138, L139); the Sustainability Report regime is explicitly tied to the base-year GHG-intensity baseline (443 kg CO2e/t crop) and to on-field emission-reduction + carbon-sequestration commitments (GHG, L126, L138, L139); and GHG-mitigating regenerative-agriculture practices are framed as the mechanism for yield increase, farm incomes, farm profitability, food security and transformation of the agricultural sector — Bayer's economic value-creation thesis across its agricultural value chain (MBV, L126, L128). Wording tolerances relaxed vs. strict Chain A spec: MBV is anchored on 'yield increase / farm incomes / farm profitability / productivity / sustainable food production system' rather than explicit 'shareholder value / market capitalisation' (Bayer's MBV narrative is intermediated through the agricultural customer base); ER is anchored on the Sustainability Report 2023 + externally-reviewed methodology + Bayer Carbon Programs + progress-reporting obligation rather than TCFD/CDP/SBTi; CG is anchored on the Sustainability Strategy chapter structure with Corporate Governance as chapter 2 and explicit executive-compensation / stakeholder-governance linkages. Edge signs match Chain A hypothesis (+, -, -). Causal markers are dense across the passage.
